subject: Re: Is dose weighting performed on tiltseries in WarpTools?
body:
Isn’t it the case that during ts preprocessing,   no dose-weighting is
applied to the 0-tilt image? (See the tomostar file - the 0 tilt will be
listed as having 0 cumulative dose)
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK


On Sun, 12 Oct 2025 at 21:58, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Maurice,
>
> Dose weighting is applied during movie export based on the per-frame dose
> supplied in your input settings file
>
> https://github.com/warpem/warp/blob/656a2d4ff1ed9ab9ecbd6b5cf9d27db8c134284a/WarpLib/Movie/Movie.ExportMovie.cs#L202-L206
>
> Dose weighting is also applied when reconstructing particle series for
> export to RELION
>
> https://github.com/warpem/warp/blob/656a2d4ff1ed9ab9ecbd6b5cf9d27db8c134284a/WarpLib/TiltSeries/TiltSeries.ReconstructParticleSeries.cs#L114-L140
>
> I remember Dimitry saying that the effects of dose weighting being applied
> twice, first to the movie and then cumulatively at the tilt series level,
> were accounted for - I assume this is true but couldn't find it quickly
> poking around the code, it's probably done in M somewhere :-)
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Sun, 12 Oct 2025 at 10:35, Maurice Frijns <m.a.frijns2000@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> I couldn't find anywhere in the documentation of WarpTools if dose
>> weighting is performed during motion correction for tilt series, I assume
>> not as the required input is not provided. Is that a correct assumption?
>> and if so, is dose weighting performed during a later step?
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Maurice
>>
>> --
